items.title: Love Island USA's executive producer James Barker has died in Fiji
items.summary_auto: James Barker, the executive producer of wildly popular television show Love Island USA, has died in Fiji.

items.title: Canada can achieve 'win-win' with U.S. if we don't 'panic and freak out' over trade threats: Smith
items.summary_auto: Alberta Premier Danielle Smith says the art of dealing with U.S. President Donald Trump is to stop panicking every time a new tariff is threatened.

items.title: Tyra Banks sues Netflix over America's Next Top Model documentary, alleging defamation
items.summary_auto: Tyra Banks has filed a defamation lawsuit against Netflix and the directors of its docuseries Reality Check: Inside America's Next Top Model, alleging that the producers stripped down hours of interview footage to construct a false narrative.

items.title: As racism allegations surface, Montréal-Nord community leaders ask police: What now?
items.summary_auto: People working with Montréal-Nord's youth say there is a pressing "urgency to act" following a police racism scandal in the borough. They are demanding a meeting with the police chief.

items.title: Gavin Newsom says Trump directed U.S. Justice Department to investigate him
items.summary_auto: California Governor Gavin Newsom on Monday accused U.S. President Donald Trump of directing the Justice Department to investigate him and his wife.

items.title: Canada cracking down on asylum claims from St-Pierre-Miquelon ferry to N.L.
items.summary_auto: The federal government says it is cracking down on what it calls a "low volume" of asylum claims made by foreigners arriving in Newfoundland and Labrador from a nearby territory of France.

items.title: Which 8-bit Nintendo games are worth playing today? This critic is playing all 751 of them to find out
items.summary_auto: Jeff Gerstmann is nearly finished playing and ranking every game released in North America for the Nintendo Entertainment System — all 751 of them.

items.title: Opioid deaths dropped 23% in 2025 — bringing 'cautious optimism' from Canada's top doctor
items.summary_auto: Opioid overdose deaths across Canada dropped by nearly a quarter last year compared to 2024, with health officials hailing the data as a sign that the federal government's drug policy is working.

items.title: World Cup contender Spain and Cape Verde play to surprising scoreless draw
items.summary_auto: An impressive performance by 40-year-old goalkeeper Vozinha helped Cape Verde play Spain, one of the favourites to win the World Cup, to a 0-0 draw on Monday. The first scoreless match is the biggest shock of the tournament so far.

items.title: Anishinaabe flutist faces financial barriers over new instrument
items.summary_auto: Tyler Evans-Knott, a 25-year-old flutist from Curve Lake First Nation, was recently accepted into McGill University’s Schulich School of Music, but had to decline the offer since he needs but can't yet afford a new instrument.

items.title: 'Greatest moment of my life': Winnipeg's Seth Jarvis gets his name on Stanley Cup
Owners of Santo Domingo nightclub to stand trial over roof collapse that killed 236
items.summary_auto: The sibling owners of the Santo Domingo nightclub where a roof collapse killed 236 people and injured more than 100 last year will stand trial for involuntary manslaughter, a judge ruled Monday.

items.title: Woman charged after car crashed into fitness studio in London, Ont., mall, injuring 7
items.summary_auto: A London, Ont., woman faces multiple criminal negligence causing bodily harm charges after a vehicle crashed into a GoodLife Fitness centre at Sherwood Forest Mall in the city's north end.

items.title: What it will take to restore global energy flow — and bring down gas prices
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump says "let the oil flow." But actually getting global oil markets back to anything close to normal will take time, money and pose an immense logistical challenge.

items.title: Building cleaner AI data centres better than not building at all, CEO behind Hamilton proposal says
items.summary_auto: The head of an Ontario tech company proposing an AI data centre for the old Hamilton Spectator building on Frid Street says he understands people's concerns about such facilities and wants to build them better.

items.title: Funeral for OPP officer killed in northern Ontario set for Thursday
items.summary_auto: The funeral for Ontario Provincial Police Const. Tarun Bali, who was killed last week, has been scheduled. Bali, 29, was struck by a vehicle in a northern Ontario town as officers carried out an investigation.

items.title: Fox agrees to buy streaming pioneer Roku for $22B US
items.summary_auto: The deal gives Fox access to more than 100 million households already using Roku, and will help the traditional TV giant push further into the world of streaming.

items.title: Calls grow for independent investigation into alleged racism at Montréal-Nord police station
items.summary_auto: Quebec's Domestic Security Minister Ian Lafrenière is appointing an independent observer to follow the investigation by Montreal police into a group of officers accused of racial profiling and discrimination during interventions in Montréal-Nord.

items.title: Global stocks soar and oil prices drop as U.S., Iran reach tentative deal to end war
items.summary_auto: Oil prices fell more than $4 US a barrel on news of a deal to end the fighting, as world markets responded positively to the developments. The S&P 500, Dow Jones Industrial Average and Nasdaq Composite all rose in early trading on Monday.

items.title: Britain to introduce legislation to ban under-16s on social media, Starmer says
items.summary_auto: Britain will ban children under 16 from using a range of social media apps including Snapchat, TikTok and YouTube to protect young people from harmful content and excessive screen time, Prime Minister Keir Starmer said on Monday.

items.title: Lebanon fighting eases after U.S.-Iran deal but displaced warned not to rush home
items.summary_auto: Fighting in southern Lebanon abated on Monday after the announcement of a U.S.-Iran deal to end the wider conflict, but local authorities warned displaced people not to rush home and Israel said it would not withdraw its troops from the area.

items.title: This Alberta village has an ambulance, but it's out of town 96% of the time
items.summary_auto: According to recent data from Emergency Health Services Alberta, the Village of Linden's ambulance has responded 11 calls within its coverage zone and 292 outside of it so far this year.

items.title: For Chinese automakers, markets like Canada are more important than ever
12-year-old driving stolen car charged after striking officer in Toronto's east end: police
items.summary_auto: A 12-year-old boy has been hospitalized and is facing charges, the Special Investigations Unit says, after he was shot at by police while driving an allegedly stolen vehicle in East York early Monday.

items.title: These teens retraced a 150-year-old river route to commemorate Treaty 6
items.summary_auto: 2026 marks 150 years since the signing of Treaty 6 — and students with the Lloydminster Public School Division are marking it in a big way: by retracing the route taken by First Nations and Canadian Crown dignitaries who entered the agreement in 1876.

items.title: Norway crown princess's son sentenced to 4 years in prison after rape conviction
items.summary_auto: Marius Borg Høiby, the eldest son of Norway's Crown Princess Mette-Marit, was sentenced to four years in prison Monday after being convicted of rape.

items.title: His colon cancer was originally misdiagnosed. Now he's encouraging screening as Ontario boosts capacity
items.summary_auto: Starting next month, Ontarians who are 45 years old will be eligible for colorectal cancer screening. Some patients and advocates question whether the province is ready for the additional pressure on the system, but Ontario says it's boosting capacity.

items.title: Manitoba will have electronic patient records that can be shared between hospitals by next spring
items.summary_auto: Every Manitoba hospital will soon begin building electronic records that will mean a patient's care history follows them from one hospital to another.

items.title: Russian attacks kill 11 across Ukraine, set centuries-old religious site in Kyiv ablaze
items.summary_auto: Russia fired hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles at Ukraine's biggest cities in a nighttime bombardment that killed at least 11 people and set fire to a renowned religious site, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and other officials said Monday.

items.title: Why Halifax is one of the toughest markets for renters, by the numbers
items.summary_auto:
Rents have been increasing at a faster pace in Halifax than in other large Canadian cities, putting pressure on affordability, says the
Canada Mortgage and Housing Corporation.

items.title: How do you get rid of 110 vats of smelly fish sauce? This Newfoundland town is finally getting an answer
items.summary_auto: It’ll take about 200 trips with a standard-sized dump truck to put an end to a stinky problem that has plagued the town of St. Mary’s, N.L., for the past 25 years.

items.title: Ottawa police have a sexual misconduct problem. A decade of fixes have largely failed
items.summary_auto: After 10 years of assessments, recommendations, promises and millions of dollars spent by the Ottawa Police Service to end sexual violence and harassment among its ranks, some current and former employees of the force tell CBC News they don't think meaningful change is coming.

items.title: Women with traumatic brain injury 26 per cent less likely to receive trauma care than men, Ontario study finds
items.summary_auto: New Ontario research shows women with traumatic brain injuries are far less likely to be admitted for trauma care than men, raising questions over why patients with similar, life-altering injuries may be receiving less-specialized treatment.

items.title: Meet the 6-year-old waiting to cross the Gordie Howe bridge after years of watching it be built
items.summary_auto: Since Lucas Legare was two years old, he's been watching history in the making with nearly weekly visits to the Gordie Howe International Bridge — an example of the building anticipation for the bridge's opening.

items.title: War, sports collide as Iran's World Cup kicks off in Los Angeles
items.summary_auto: A divisive collision of geopolitics and sports is playing out in a community with emotional ties to two countries at war, setting the stage for drama and protest that will go beyond the pitch.

items.title: Guitars, medication and heating pads: How these musicians manage chronic illness on the road
items.summary_auto: After her music blew up during lockdown, Canadian indie musician Hannah Judge, a.k.a. fanclubwallet, says it's been challenging to navigate a music career and touring while managing the symptoms of Crohn's disease. She explores how she and other musicians do it in a short documentary for CBC Ottawa’s Creator Network.

items.title: Iran, U.S. have a deal of sorts. What happens next?
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump and senior Iranian officials say they have reached a memorandum of understanding to bring an end to the war pitting the United States and Israel against Iran. Here's what we know about the deal so far.

items.title: Pro-separation billboard still up after removal deadline from Alberta town passes
items.summary_auto: Cory Morgan says the sign he paid for is still standing in Taber, Alta., in defiance of a letter he says the town sent earlier this month to the private owner of the billboard demanding the message be removed by Saturday.
